pattern: Same 3-D DP as variant I but constraints up to 1000 demand the O(zero*one) prefix-sum formulation be applied correctly for both axes.
hints: The recurrence is identical to variant I: f0[i][j] = sum f1[i-r][j] for r=1..min(i,limit) and f1[i][j] = sum f0[i][j-r] for r=1..min(j,limit).
With limit up to 1000 and zero/one up to 1000, a naive O(z*o*limit) triple loop would be 10^9 operations. Use prefix sums over rows for f1 and over columns for f0 to reduce each cell to O(1).
Build p1[i][j] = sum f1[0..i][j] row by row; when computing f0[i][j], use p1[i-1][j] - p1[i-limit-1][j]. Similarly maintain a column prefix for f0 within each row to get f1[i][j] in O(1).

edge_inputs.description: limit covers entire length no restriction
large equal counts tight limit 1 - strict alternation only one arrangement

stress.description: random medium parameters within variant II range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    zero = rng.randint(1, size)
    one = rng.randint(1, size)
    limit = rng.randint(1, size)
    return [zero, one, limit]
